lessons.text: Do your duty without clinging to results.
lessons.rationale: Defines the true renouncer as one who acts without depending on fruits, not one who merely abstains.

lessons.text: Renounce selfish intentions, not action itself.
lessons.rationale: Yoga is renunciation of self-centered thoughts and attachment, reached through action then settling into inaction.

lessons.text: Lift yourself by your own effort.
lessons.rationale: Raise yourself by your own self; do not let yourself sink.

lessons.text: Be your own friend, not your own enemy.
lessons.rationale: The conquered self befriends you; the unconquered self acts as your foe.

lessons.text: Stay balanced through cold and heat, praise and blame.
lessons.rationale: The self-controlled remain even in pleasure and pain, honor and dishonor.

lessons.text: Value wisdom over gold and stone alike.
lessons.rationale: The content yogi treats a clod, a stone, and gold as the same.

lessons.text: Look on friend and foe with the same eye.
lessons.rationale: Excellence is being even-minded toward the loving, the hostile, and the indifferent.

lessons.text: Practice in solitude, free from hope and greed.
lessons.rationale: Strive constantly to steady the mind, alone, with body and mind controlled.

lessons.text: Set a firm, clean seat, neither high nor low.
lessons.rationale: Practical instruction on preparing a stable place to sit.

lessons.text: Make the mind one-pointed to purify yourself.
lessons.rationale: Seated, gather the mind and senses to a single point for inner purification.

lessons.text: Hold your body, head, and neck straight and still.
lessons.rationale: Concrete posture instruction: erect, steady, gaze unwandering.

lessons.text: Sit serene and fearless, mind fixed on the goal.
lessons.rationale: Calm, fearless, disciplined, with the highest as the supreme aim.

lessons.text: A steadied mind leads you to lasting peace.
lessons.rationale: Keeping the mind balanced culminates in the peace of liberation.

lessons.text: Eat and sleep in moderation, never in extremes.
lessons.rationale: Yoga is impossible with too much or too little food and sleep; moderation destroys pain.

lessons.text: Rest the mind in the Self, free of craving.
lessons.rationale: Union is the controlled mind resting in the Self, free from all longing.

lessons.text: Keep your mind unflickering like a sheltered lamp.
lessons.rationale: The steady yogi's mind is like a flame where no wind blows.

lessons.text: Grow content by seeing the Self within yourself.
lessons.rationale: When the mind quiets, one sees the Self by the Self and is satisfied.

lessons.text: Anchor yourself in bliss beyond the senses.
lessons.rationale: Infinite bliss grasped by the intellect, transcending the senses, holds one steady.

lessons.text: Once you find it, no sorrow can shake you.
lessons.rationale: Established in this gain, one is unmoved even by heavy grief.

lessons.text: Practice with resolve and an undespairing mind.
lessons.rationale: Yoga, the severance from pain, is to be practiced with determination and without despair.

lessons.text: Let go of every desire born of imagination.
lessons.rationale: Abandon all desires from mental fancy and restrain the senses from every side.

lessons.text: Quiet the mind gradually, one step at a time.
lessons.rationale: Little by little, steady the intellect and fix the mind on the Self alone.

lessons.text: Whenever the mind wanders, gently bring it back.
lessons.rationale: From wherever the restless mind strays, draw it back under the Self's control.

lessons.text: A calmed mind opens the door to supreme bliss.
lessons.rationale: The yogi of peaceful mind, free from passion, enjoys the infinite bliss of Brahman.

lessons.text: See yourself in all beings and all beings in you.
lessons.rationale: The harmonized mind sees the Self everywhere and never feels separate.

lessons.text: Worship the one presence dwelling in all beings.
lessons.rationale: Established in unity, the yogi abides in the divine present in all.

lessons.text: Feel others' joy and pain as your own.
lessons.rationale: The highest yogi sees all through the likeness of the Self, in pleasure and pain alike.

lessons.text: Admit the mind is restless and hard to hold.
lessons.rationale: Arjuna names the mind restless, turbulent, and as hard to control as the wind.

lessons.text: Tame the restless mind through practice and detachment.
lessons.rationale: Krishna's answer: the difficult mind is restrained by practice and dispassion.

lessons.text: Self-mastery makes the seemingly impossible attainable.
lessons.rationale: Yoga is hard for the uncontrolled but reachable by the striving, self-controlled one.

lessons.text: No sincere effort toward good is ever wasted.
lessons.rationale: The one who falls from yoga does not perish; none who does good comes to grief.

lessons.text: Every good beginning carries forward into your future.
lessons.rationale: The fallen yogi is reborn favorably and reconnects with former practice, striving further.

lessons.text: Persist across setbacks; progress compounds over time.
lessons.rationale: The yogi who strives on, perfected gradually, reaches the highest goal.

lessons.text: Choose disciplined practice over mere knowledge or ritual.
lessons.rationale: The yogi surpasses ascetics, scholars, and ritualists; devoted practice with faith is highest.
